step_rationale: Atherogenic LDL subclass; retained in intima; potent substrate for oxidation and plaque formation.
mechanism: Small dense LDL particles are highly atherogenic, readily retained in the arterial intima, and oxidized to promote atherosclerotic plaque initiation.

references.title: The Role of Lipids in Atherosclerosis: Focus on Molecular Biology Mechanisms and Therapeutic Approaches.
Atherogenic Lipoproteins for the Statin Residual Cardiovascular Disease Risk.
Hepatic Lipase: a Comprehensive View of its Role on Plasma Lipid and Lipoprotein Metabolism.
[Dyslipidemia and atherosclerosis].
references.journal: Nihon rinsho. Japanese journal of clinical medicine

references.title: Prevention of a first myocardial infarction.
